title: An Ode For Ben Jonson
content:
Ah Ben!
Say how, or when
Shall we thy guests
Meet at those lyric feasts
Made at the Sun,
The Dog, the Triple Tun?
Where we such clusters had
As made us nobly wild, not mad;
And yet each verse of thine
Outdid the meat, outdid the frolic wine.
My Ben
Or come again,
Or send to us
Thy wit's great overplus;
But teach us yet
Wisely to husband it;
Lest we that talent spend,
And having once brought to an end
That precious stock, the store
Of such a wit the world should have no more.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "An Ode For Ben Jonson"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
